mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate below 0.5% signals an ultra-loose monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset price multiples. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the rate remains in the sub-0.5% zone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% indicates extreme monetary accommodation
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any rolling 2-year window in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate above 9.5% signals labor market slack and recessionary pressure, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days when unemployment crosses above 9.5%.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.2 over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deterioration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating late-cycle contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both series decline month-over-month simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Both demand indicators at moderate levels (RSAFS 355546M, HOUST 591K)
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) above 101.0 signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity growth, supporting corporate profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days when INDPRO remains above the 101.0 threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 100.9862 is near but below 101.0 threshold
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 54% of observations when INDPRO > 101.0, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50K new jobs per month, signaling labor market momentum loss and forward earnings pressure. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when monthly payroll growth falls below this critical level.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 129526K indicates current employment level
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 56% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.15 over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Interaction between elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) and restrictive monetary policy (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals a tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days when both conditions hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.587 is below 220 threshold; FEDFUNDS at 0.13% is far below 4.0%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth and multiple expansion. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during periods of above-trend GDP growth, with the effect strongest in mid-cycle expansions.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13161.1B (chained 2017 dollars) reflects current economic output
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 57% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.18 over any 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 550K units signals deteriorating residential investment and forward consumption weakness, as housing is a leading indicator of household balance sheet health. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days when housing starts fall below this critical level.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal produces a Sharpe ratio below 0.12 over any 3-year walk-forward window.
